section_name: Person Arrested to Be Taken Before Magistrate
text:
(a) A person arrested for a violation of this subtitle punishable as a misdemeanor shall be immediately taken before a magistrate if:

(1) the person is arrested on a charge of failure to stop in the event of a collision causing damage to property; or

(2) the person demands an immediate appearance before a magistrate or refuses to make a written promise to appear in court as provided by this subchapter.

(b) The person must be taken before a magistrate who:

(1) has jurisdiction of the offense;

(2) is in the county in which the offense charged is alleged to have been committed; and

(3) is nearest or most accessible to the place of arrest.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 543. Arrest & Prosecution of Violators
##### § 543.002 — Person Arrested to Be Taken Before Magistrate

(a) A person arrested for a violation of this subtitle punishable as a misdemeanor shall be immediately taken before a magistrate if:

    (1) the person is arrested on a charge of failure to stop in the event of a collision causing damage to property; or

    (2) the person demands an immediate appearance before a magistrate or refuses to make a written promise to appear in court as provided by this subchapter.

(b) The person must be taken before a magistrate who:

    (1) has jurisdiction of the offense;

    (2) is in the county in which the offense charged is alleged to have been committed; and

    (3) is nearest or most accessible to the place of arrest.

*Acts 2023, 88th Leg., R.S., Ch. 709 (H.B.2190), Sec. 31, eff. September 1, 2023.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A person arrested for a violation of this subtitle punishable as a misdemeanor shall be immediately taken before a magistrate if:
the person is arrested on a charge of failure to stop in the event of a collision causing damage to property; or
the person demands an immediate appearance before a magistrate or refuses to make a written promise to appear in court as provided by this subchapter.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The person must be taken before a magistrate who:
is in the county in which the offense charged is alleged to have been committed; and
is nearest or most accessible to the place of arrest.

history.raw: Acts 2023, 88th Leg., R.S., Ch. 709 (H.B.2190), Sec. 31, eff. September 1, 2023.
